title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O 2026 with Frontier Agentic Performance
summary: Google debuted Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O 2026, its fastest and most capable agentic model yet, now the default across the Gemini app and Google Search globally.
content: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O 2026, the first model in its new 3.5 family designed around agentic workflows and long-horizon task execution. The model outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on challenging coding and agentic benchmarks — scoring 76.2% on Terminal-Bench 2.1 — and is four times faster than competing frontier models on output tokens per second. It is now the default model for the Gemini app and Google Search globally and powers the new Gemini Spark personal AI agent, which runs 24/7 to take actions on users' behalf across connected Google apps. Enterprise partners including Shopify, Salesforce, Macquarie Bank, and Xero are already piloting 3.5 Flash for complex multi-week automated workflows. Gemini 3.5 Pro, the heavier-weight sibling, is in internal testing and expected to launch publicly next month.

title: Andrej Karpathy Joins Anthropic to Use Claude to Build the Next Claude
summary: OpenAI co-founder Andrej Karpathy has joined Anthropic's pre-training team with a recursive mandate: use Claude itself to speed up the research that produces the next version of Claude.
content: Andrej Karpathy, one of OpenAI's 11 original co-founders and the creator of the widely followed 'Neural Networks: Zero to Hero' course, announced he has joined Anthropic's pre-training team under team lead Nick Joseph. His mandate is to build a new group focused on a strikingly recursive goal: using Claude to accelerate the most compute-intensive phase of frontier model development, which is the single most expensive step in building systems like Claude. Karpathy posted on X that he believes 'the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative,' drawing 13.6 million views. The hire is a significant talent coup for Anthropic, which is valued at roughly $800 billion and reportedly exploring an IPO as early as late 2026. The move comes as OpenAI has experienced over a dozen high-profile departures in the past two years, including its CTO Mira Murati and reinforcement learning pioneer John Schulman.

title: Governor Newsom Signs First-in-Nation Executive Order to Protect Workers from AI Disruption
summary: California Governor Gavin Newsom signed a first-in-the-nation executive order mobilizing state agencies, universities, and industry leaders to prepare workers and small businesses for AI-driven economic disruption.
content: California Governor Gavin Newsom signed an executive order directing state agencies to develop policies protecting workers from AI-driven economic disruption — the first order of its kind in any US state. The order explores policies including severance standards, employment insurance for displaced workers, worker ownership models, expanded workforce training, and stronger tracking of hiring and payroll trends to detect layoff signals early. A new statewide dashboard will track AI's impact across industries, and the state's WARN Act will be reviewed within 180 days to ensure it provides timely warning data about AI-driven layoffs. The order coincides with the launch of Engaged California, a first-ever statewide deliberative democracy initiative inviting all Californians to shape AI policy at engaged.ca.gov/ai. California is home to 33 of the world's top 50 private AI companies, and Newsom framed the order as a necessary step to ensure workers — not just tech companies — share in the productivity gains AI generates.
source: Office of the Governor of California

title: Trump Set to Sign AI Oversight Order Requiring 90-Day Pre-Release Access for Federal Agencies
summary: President Trump is expected to sign an executive order establishing a voluntary framework under which AI developers would give federal agencies and critical infrastructure operators 90 days of pre-release access to powerful frontier models.
content: President Donald Trump is preparing to sign an executive order establishing a voluntary pre-release disclosure framework requiring AI developers to share access to new frontier models with the federal government 90 days before public launch. The order would give cybersecurity agencies — including CISA and the NSA's Cybersecurity Collaboration Center — early access to evaluate security risks, alongside critical-infrastructure operators such as banks, energy companies, and major healthcare networks. The voluntary structure is a compromise: industry advisers led by David Sacks pushed back against mandatory disclosure on competitiveness grounds, while MAGA-aligned national security voices including Steve Bannon and Amy Kremer pressed for harder requirements. No civil or criminal penalties apply to non-participants, though declining is framed as a reputational risk that the government can publicly call out. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta, and Microsoft are all expected to participate from launch, with the first disclosed model expected before the end of Q3 2026.

title: OpenAI Opens First International Applied AI Lab in Singapore with S$300M Commitment
summary: OpenAI announced its first Applied AI Lab outside the US in Singapore, backed by over S$300 million and a plan to create more than 200 local technical roles.
content: OpenAI announced the launch of its first Applied AI Lab outside the United States in Singapore, backed by a commitment of more than S$300 million (approximately US$225 million) in partnership with the Ministry of Digital Development and Information. The initiative, called OpenAI for Singapore, was revealed at the ATx Summit and will create over 200 Singapore-based technical roles, with Singapore becoming one of OpenAI's global hubs for forward-deployed engineers helping organizations deploy AI across public service, finance, and digital infrastructure. The company will also run OpenAI Academy programs, Codex for Teachers hackathons, and accelerator workshops for local startups and SMEs. Coinciding with the announcement, Singapore's Infocomm Media Development Authority updated its agentic AI governance framework — originally launched at Davos in January 2026 — incorporating input from over 60 organizations including AWS, DBS, Google, Tencent, and Salesforce. The revised framework adds guidance on multi-agent system risks, automation bias, and human accountability, with over ten real-world case studies from enterprise deployments.

title: Cohere Releases Command A+ as First Fully Apache 2.0 Open-Source Enterprise AI Model
summary: Cohere released Command A+, a 218-billion-parameter model under a fully permissive Apache 2.0 license, letting any organization run and commercialize it without licensing restrictions.
content: Canadian AI lab Cohere unveiled Command A+, a 218-billion-parameter sparse mixture-of-experts model released under an Apache 2.0 open-source license — its first fully permissive release, allowing any organization to use, modify, and commercialize the model with no licensing fees or non-compete clauses. The model's key technical breakthrough is lossless 4-bit quantization: by keeping attention pathways at full precision while compressing only its MoE expert layers, Cohere achieved a model that runs on a single NVIDIA B200 GPU at 375 tokens per second with minimal quality degradation. Command A+ introduces native citation generation, automatically linking every factual claim to its source document — a feature critical for regulated industries like finance, healthcare, and legal. Benchmark improvements are dramatic: the model jumped from 37% to 85% on complex reasoning tasks and from 57% to 90% on AIME 25 math. The release advances a growing 'sovereign AI' push, enabling enterprises to deploy frontier-grade intelligence on their own private or air-gapped infrastructure, completely independent of vendor cloud infrastructure.
